meta.target_buyer: VP Marketing / Head of Marketing / Founder

score.label: Good
score.interpretation: Good — minor friction in comparison layer

diagnosis.headline: Homepage is strong — buyers can land, make sense, and commit. Comparison layer needs work.
diagnosis.text: This is a well-built homepage. Pain is clear, process is visible, proof is strong. The gaps are in the comparison layer: buyers do not know what they're switching from (DIY? Freelancer? Agency?) and urgency is weak. These gaps create friction for skeptical stakeholders but won't break the buying path.

reply_rate.basis: Based on Q3 (pain clear) + Q6 (ICP defined)

demo_conversion.basis: Based on Q13 (process visible) + Q11-12 (proof strong)

internal_sell.basis: Based on Q8 (alternative unclear) + Q22 (ROI scattered)

pipeline_speed.basis: Based on Q5 (urgency weak) + Q9 (no why-not-X)

predictions.summary: Buyers will respond and convert to calls. Deals may slow when CFO asks 'why not just hire a freelancer?' or 'where's the ROI case?'

recognition: How is this different from hiring a freelance PMM or agency?
What happens if we don't love the positioning options you present?
Can you show me a before/after for a company in our specific industry?
Is this the right time, or should we wait until we're bigger / have more traction?

message_priority.evidence: Pain-first hero: 'Let's fix your confusing positioning.' Buyers immediately see the problem Fletch solves. No category-first confusion.

buyer_focus.evidence: 'B2B software companies' is clear. Pricing by stage ($10K/$20K/$30K) helps buyers self-select. ICP is well-defined.

why_act_now.evidence: 'Booking in April' creates scarcity but no cost of waiting. Buyers can defer because there's no 'this costs you X' framing.

proof_relevance.evidence: Quantified results: '25% conversion increase', '54% ARR growth', '5% to 20% conversion'. Same-role testimonials (CMOs, VPs, Founders). Recognizable logos.

explanation_burden.evidence: 'Product marketing consultancy' is a known category. 'Homepage Positioning Sprint' is immediately understandable. No invented terms.

internal_sharing.evidence: If champion forwards this link, second reader can understand value from before/afters and testimonials. Clear pricing helps budget conversations.

mixed_signals.evidence: Consistent consulting motion throughout. Pricing is visible. Process is detailed. No contradictions between positioning and offer shape.

breakpoint.insight: Buyers get the value but cannot easily compare Fletch to alternatives. The homepage never names what buyers are switching from — DIY? Freelancer? Agency? Internal PMM hire? This creates objection-handling work for sales and gives skeptical stakeholders ammunition.

missing_items.text: What am I switching from? (DIY, freelancer, agency not named)
Why do alternatives fail? (No explicit comparison)
Cost of waiting with bad positioning (urgency missing)

decisions.name: Alternative
decisions.finding: What is buyer switching from? DIY positioning? Freelance PMM? Agency? Internal hire? None are named. Skeptical CFO will ask 'why not just hire someone?'

decisions.name: Why Not Wait
decisions.finding: 'Booking in April' creates scarcity, but no cost of inaction. What does bad positioning cost per month? Per quarter? Not quantified.

decisions.name: Risk Reversal
decisions.finding: 'Chat' CTA is low-commitment, but no explicit 'what if it doesn't work' framing. FAQ addresses some objections but no guarantee language visible.

recommendations.eyebrow: Light Refinement — Not a Major Rebuild
recommendations.action: This homepage is already strong. Three targeted additions would close the gaps:

goals.label: Name the Alternative
goals.description: Add a section addressing 'Why Fletch vs. DIY / Freelancer / Agency / Internal Hire?' — preempt the comparison question.

goals.label: Quantify Cost of Waiting
goals.description: Add urgency — 'Every month with unclear positioning costs you X% conversion' or 'Deals stall when buyers can't explain your value.'

goals.label: Add Risk Reversal
goals.description: Consider explicit language about what happens if the positioning doesn't land — even a light 'we iterate until you're confident' would help.

evidence.question: What is this?
evidence.inference: Clear category. Buyers know what they're looking at.

evidence.question: What do you do?
evidence.quote: helped 500+ B2B software companies find their positioning strategy...
evidence.inference: Core function clearly stated.

evidence.question: Pain worth switching?
evidence.inference: Pain named in hero. Checklist reinforces symptoms.

evidence.question: What project?
evidence.inference: Named deliverable. 2-week timeline. Clear project scope.

evidence.question: Why act now?
evidence.inference: Scarcity exists but no cost of waiting quantified.

evidence.question: For my team?
evidence.quote: B2B software companies + pricing by stage
evidence.inference: Clear segment. Tiered pricing helps self-selection.

evidence.question: For my situation?
evidence.quote: Do you have a positioning problem? checklist
evidence.inference: Interactive checklist helps buyers diagnose fit.

evidence.question: Switching from?
evidence.inference: No 'instead of DIY' or 'instead of agency' framing.

evidence.question: Why alternatives fail?
evidence.inference: Why DIY fails, why agencies fail — none covered.

evidence.question: What's different?
evidence.inference: Experience + speed implied. Not positioned against specific alternative.

evidence.question: Teams like mine?
evidence.quote: Superhuman, Notion, Synthesia, Lemlist, PagerDuty, GitLab
evidence.inference: Strong logos. Recognizable B2B SaaS companies.

evidence.question: Results achieved?
evidence.quote: 25% increase in homepage conversion, 54% ARR growth
evidence.inference: Quantified outcomes with named people.

evidence.question: How much effort?
evidence.inference: Time commitment extremely clear.

evidence.question: Proof match role?
evidence.quote: CMO, CEO, VP Marketing, Head of Marketing, Founder
evidence.inference: Same-role testimonials. Target buyer roles represented.

evidence.question: Enterprise signals?
evidence.inference: Enterprise logos suggest capability. Consulting doesn't need security page.

evidence.question: First step?
evidence.inference: Clear CTA. Books directly to calendar.

evidence.question: Safe to try?
evidence.inference: Chat is low-commitment. No explicit risk reversal.

evidence.question: After I click?
evidence.inference: Standard booking flow. Pre-engagement process visible.

evidence.question: CTA match readiness?
evidence.inference: Appropriate for consulting motion.

evidence.question: Offer match motion?
evidence.inference: Clear pricing. Matches consulting motion.

evidence.question: Explore without sales?
evidence.inference: Some depth content. No ungated guide or framework.

evidence.question: ROI documentation?
evidence.inference: ROI scattered in quotes. No systematic framework.

indicators.what: 'How is this different from hiring a freelancer?' asked on calls
Deals that stall at budget approval
indicators.direction: Should decrease after urgency framing added

indicators.what: Time from first touch to signed contract
indicators.direction: Should shorten as comparison layer strengthens
